metadata.summary: JavaScript/TypeScript testing stack: Vitest vs Jest decision, component testing, MSW for API mocking, and ESM/CJS pitfalls.

content.title: Testing in JavaScript
content.summary: Covers the Vitest vs Jest decision tree (2025), unit and component testing patterns, React Testing Library integration, MSW v2 for API mocking, fake timers, coverage configuration, and common pitfalls (ESM/CJS confusion, jsdom layout limitations, MSW v1→v2 migration, fake timer leaks).

why.p: The JS testing ecosystem fragmented between Jest (CommonJS-native) and Vitest (ESM-native, Vite-integrated). Choosing the wrong runner or misconfiguring the environment causes non-deterministic test results and hard-to-debug import errors.

list.item: Setting up a new JavaScript/TypeScript test suite (Vitest or Jest)
Writing unit tests for React components with React Testing Library
Mocking HTTP calls with MSW (v2 API)
Debugging ESM/CJS import resolution errors in tests
Configuring fake timers without leaking state
General mocking strategy decisions — use mocking-strategies

section.name: runner-selection
section.title: Vitest vs Jest: Runner Selection
section.p: Decision tree for choosing Vitest or Jest, ESM/CJS handling differences, config comparison, and migration guide.

rule.statement: Use Vitest when: project uses Vite, Vue, Svelte, or modern ESM-first setup.
Use Jest when: project uses Create React App, Next.js (without Vitest plugin), or has deep Jest ecosystem dependencies.
For new TypeScript projects with no existing setup: default to Vitest.

list.item: Vitest: native ESM, Vite config reuse, 10-20x faster HMR, vi.mock() API
Jest: battle-tested, jsdom by default, broader plugin ecosystem, CRA/Next.js built-in

rule.statement: Vitest handles ESM natively — no transform config needed for modern packages.
Jest requires transform configuration for ESM packages — add to transformIgnorePatterns.

antipattern.description: Jest fails on ESM-only package (e.g., chalk v5, node-fetch v3)
antipattern.reason: Jest uses CommonJS by default. ESM-only packages must be transformed. Vitest avoids this entirely.

example.title: Vitest config (vitest.config.ts)
Jest config (jest.config.ts)

list.item: Remove babel-jest if using Vite — Vitest uses Vite's transform pipeline

section.name: test-patterns
section.title: JavaScript/TypeScript Test Patterns
section.p: AAA for JS, async patterns (waitFor, findBy), hooks testing (useState, useEffect), fake timers, and coverage configuration.

rule.statement: Use blank lines to separate the three sections, same as Python.
Always return or await async test functions — silent unhandled rejections mask failures.
Use findBy* queries (async) instead of getBy* + waitFor for elements that appear after async operations.
Use vi.useFakeTimers() / jest.useFakeTimers() for time-dependent code. Always restore in afterEach.
Call vi.runAllTimers() or vi.advanceTimersByTime() to trigger scheduled callbacks.
Use v8 provider for Vitest (native, fast). Use istanbul for Jest (more detailed branch coverage).
Set thresholds for lines, functions, branches in config — fail CI if they drop.

section.name: msw-and-components
section.title: MSW v2 and Component Testing
section.p: MSW v2 setup for Node (tests) and browser (dev), React Testing Library query priority, component test structure, and jsdom limitations.

rule.statement: MSW v2 changed the API significantly from v1 — use http.get() not rest.get(), and HttpResponse not res(ctx...).
Use onUnhandledRequest: 'error' to surface missed mock configurations early.
Query priority (most to least preferred): getByRole → getByLabelText → getByPlaceholderText → getByText → getByTestId.
Only use getByTestId as a last resort — it couples tests to implementation details.
Test user behavior, not implementation details — do not test state, refs, or component methods directly.
jsdom does not support layout (offsetWidth, getBoundingClientRect always returns 0). Use Playwright for visual/layout tests.
jsdom does not support window.matchMedia — mock it in setup.ts if your code uses it.

template.purpose: Vitest config with coverage, jsdom, path aliases
MSW v2 server setup for Node (tests) and browser (dev)
